summary: Iteration 7 — Comprehensive test of P0 (i18n parity for kpiSharpeShort + Performance keys) + P1 (CSV export/import, Recharts equity curve, SimulatorPro refactor) for Trading Calculator PRO. Backend: 7/7 pytest pass (auth, bulk trades CRUD, options payoff spec-exact regression, AAPL stock regression). Frontend: Login + token persistence confirmed (stored at localStorage['btc-auth-storage']). Performance Journal tab renders with all 3 data-testids. CSV import works end-to-end (toast 'X trades imported correctly', table populates, Analytics reflects new trades). Analytics tab renders Recharts AreaChart (CURVA DE CAPITAL, 1 recharts-area element), all 8 KPI cards present (WIN RATE, PROFIT FACTOR, ESPERANZA MAT, R MEDIO with 'Sharpe 1.67' subValue, P&L TOTAL, MAX DRAWDOWN, CUMPLIMIENTO REGLAS, RACHAS). No raw i18n keys (kpiSharpeShort/perfTab/journalCsv) leak in either ES or EN. i18n switcher verified: EN shows 'Trading Journal'/'New Trade'/'Export CSV'/'Import CSV'. SimulatorPro: container + all 5 config data-testids (initial-balance, total-phases, mode-compound, mode-fixed, execute-simulation-btn) present; execute produces simulation-results with final-balance='$1,487.04'; new-simulation-btn correctly resets (results disappear). Auto-cleanup: 2 test trades (setup='csv-test-imported') deleted after test run.

integration_issues.issue: Playwright expect_download did not capture the file download event, and monkey-patched URL.createObjectURL + HTMLAnchorElement.prototype.click both returned null after clicking [data-testid="trade-journal-export"] with 2 existing trades visible in the table. Download button is enabled (not disabled) and loads without console error. The lib/csv.js downloadFile uses Blob + createObjectURL + transient <a> click which IS the standard pattern. Possible: the handler early-returns before calling downloadFile when some precondition fails (e.g., translation key missing, filename computed empty, async data fetch). MAIN AGENT: please verify handleExport in TradeImportExport.jsx actually reaches downloadFile — add a console.log or check the handler branches for empty-data guards / try-catch swallowing. The import path works perfectly so the half-feature is unusable without export.

design_issues.screen: Header on /dashboard after fresh login (English UI)
issues: Header sometimes renders in English ('Dashboard / Options / Performance / Pricing / Education') on initial load after login, even though the review states Spanish is the default. On /performance the subtabs correctly show Spanish (Diario de Trading, Analytics). Inconsistent: suggests initial auth-store hydration happens with language=en before the stored preference flips to es. Not blocking — toggling via globe icon works in both directions.

action_items: Investigate why [data-testid="trade-journal-export"] click does not trigger lib/csv.js downloadFile (URL.createObjectURL never called). Check TradeImportExport.jsx handleExport for: empty trades guard, async await on trade fetch, try/catch hiding errors, or using the wrong filename template.
(Nit) Ensure initial language on dashboard after login is ES (Spanish) by default — currently renders EN header then the switcher may fix it.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/performance/trades/bulk: robust per-row enrichment — single bad row does NOT block the batch (tested via empty list returns {imported:0, failed:[]}). Good error isolation.
GET /api/performance/trades returns {trades:[], count:N} envelope — frontend should destructure this; main agent has confirmed compatibility.
Options payoff returns breakEvens at TOP level of the response (not inside stats) — the review statement 'breakEvens includes 155.0' is correct; test adjusted accordingly.
SimulatorPro refactor: thin orchestrator pattern (139-line container + 4 focused children) is clean; no regression observed in execute → results → new-simulation-btn lifecycle.
CSV import uses PUT-through-POST-bulk which preserves setup tag — confirmed 'csv-test-imported' rows appear in Analytics 'POR SETUP' breakdown with correct percentages.
Auth token stored under localStorage['btc-auth-storage'] (zustand persist) instead of a plain 'token' key — any external tooling (e.g., tests) must read the nested state.token field.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (login+token persist, journal tabs, CSV import, Analytics Recharts+KPIs, i18n EN/ES parity, SimulatorPro execute+reset all pass; only CSV export click-handler wiring is not reaching downloadFile)

seed_data_creation: Created 2 trades via CSV import (TESTSYM1, TESTSYM2) with setup='csv-test-imported' during UI testing; all deleted via /api/performance/trades/{id} DELETE at end of run. Server state restored to 0 trades.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend endpoints (auth, performance bulk/list/delete, options payoff, stock) work. Demo seed does NOT include TSLA+AAPL trades the review claims — journal was empty on login. Auth token stored at localStorage['btc-auth-storage'] (zustand), not 'token'. Export button renders and is enabled, but clicking it does NOT invoke URL.createObjectURL — likely a broken/guard-exited handler in TradeImportExport.jsx. Recharts warnings about width=-1/height=-1 appear on Analytics but chart DOES render after a second tick (use wait_for_timeout(3000) minimum before screenshot). SimulatorPro reset works via [data-testid="new-simulation-btn"]. i18n switcher globe icon is in the header; clicking it shows a dropdown with 'English'/'Español'/etc.
rca of the issue: CSV Export button does not trigger a download. Reproduction: login as demo → /performance → Diario de Trading tab → ensure ≥1 trade visible → click [data-testid="trade-journal-export"]. Expected: a CSV file 'trade-journal-YYYY-MM-DD.csv' downloads. Actual: nothing happens. Monkey-patching URL.createObjectURL before the click captures nothing (window.__downloadCaptured === null), meaning the handler short-circuits before calling lib/csv.js::downloadFile. No console error. Suspects in TradeImportExport.jsx handleExport: (a) async fetch of trades fails silently with try/catch and returns before toCsv/downloadFile, (b) empty-data guard fires even though 2 rows are visible in the table (stale state), (c) handler reads from a different store/prop than the one feeding the table. Mitigation: add console.log at entry + before downloadFile call in handleExport; confirm trades prop length > 0 at click time; remove any try/catch that swallows.
